backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor for the Pokemon game backend, handling user profiles, Pokemon/battle types, and mixing in blob storage and authorization modules.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping implemented game features (Professor Oak intro, starter selection, battle system, strategy input) to their source files, components, and test IDs.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document providing one-line summaries of each frontend source file, describing their roles and notable bug fixes applied.
frontend/index.css: Global CSS file that imports Tailwind directives and defines CSS custom properties for light and dark theme color tokens used throughout the app.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the React app titled 'Ash's Pokemon Journey', mounting the app into a root div and loading the main TSX module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Main React application entry point that configures TanStack Router with all page routes (login, intro, starter-selection, game, battle, story, gyms, roster, profile) under a shared Layout.
Reusable component that renders a Pokemon sprite or emoji with configurable size, animation class, glow effect, sparkle particles, and optional horizontal flip.
Component that plays a three-phase attack animation (launch, impact, done) with a flying emoji projectile, explosion, and screen flash effect.
frontend/src/components/BadgeDisplay.tsx: Component that fetches earned gym badges and displays all eight gym badge slots in a grid, highlighting earned badges and showing a count summary.
frontend/src/components/EvolutionModal.t: A modal component that animates and handles Pokémon evolution, showing stat comparisons before and after, then calling a mutation and invoking a callback with the evolved Pokémon data.
frontend/src/components/HealthBar.tsx: Renders a Pokémon health bar UI element with color-coded fill (green/yellow/red) based on current HP percentage, displaying name, level, and HP numbers.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Main app shell component providing a sticky header with logo and logout, a bottom navigation bar for game routes, and a footer, while rendering child routes via Outlet.
frontend/src/components/PokemonBattleSpr: Renders a Pokémon battle sprite (image or emoji) with animation classes based on state (idle, attack, hit, faint, dodge), a drop shadow, and a colored name tag.
frontend/src/components/StrategyInput.ts: A text input component where players type battle strategies; it matches keywords to predefined responses and passes the strategy and matched keywords to a parent callback.
frontend/src/components/TeamRocketEncoun: Displays a Team Rocket encounter modal that plays through their motto line by line, then offers Battle or Flee buttons with a blast-off animation on flee.
frontend/src/data/pokemonData.ts: Defines TypeScript interfaces and static data for local Pokémon and moves, including Pikachu and the three starter Pokémon with their stats, sprites, and move sets.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Creates and caches an ICP backend actor using React Query, returning either an authenticated or anonymous actor based on the current Internet Identity, and invalidates dependent queries when the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an InternetIdentityProvider and useInternetIdentity hook for managing ICP Internet Identity authentication state, including login, logout, and identity persistence via AuthClient.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Exports React Query hooks for all backend interactions including user profile, Pokémon, badges, story arc, and battle log CRUD operations using the actor from useActor.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet that imports Google Fonts, configures Tailwind CSS, and defines light/dark theme CSS variables plus custom utility classes for anime/Pokémon-themed UI animations and styles.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider wrapping the root App component.
frontend/src/pages/Battle.tsx: Full-featured Pokémon battle page component managing turn-based combat state, attack animations, dodge mechanics, strategy input, and persisting battle results to the backend via mutations.
frontend/src/pages/GameHome.tsx: Main game hub page shown after authentication, displaying the trainer's profile, badges, starter Pokémon, quick battle navigation, and a random Team Rocket encounter modal.
frontend/src/pages/GymBattles.tsx: Displays a list of gym leaders with a challenge modal showing gym details, difficulty, and badge rewards, then navigates to the battle screen upon confirmation.
frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx: Renders the login page with Internet Identity authentication, redirecting authenticated users to the intro or game page based on whether they have an existing profile.
frontend/src/pages/PokemonRoster.tsx: Shows the player's Pokémon roster loaded from sessionStorage, allowing evolution via evolution stones through an EvolutionModal component.
frontend/src/pages/ProfessorOakIntro.tsx: Presents a typewriter-style dialogue sequence with Professor Oak introducing the Pokémon world, then navigates to the starter selection screen when complete.
frontend/src/pages/StarterSelection.tsx: Lets the player choose a starter Pokémon from a grid, stores the selection in sessionStorage, and navigates to the game page after a confirmation animation.
frontend/src/pages/StoryCampaign.tsx: React page for the story campaign mode, displaying story arcs and episodes with lock/completion states, cinematic cutscenes for final episodes, and navigation to battles with appropriate backgrounds.
frontend/src/pages/TrainerProfile.tsx: React page rendering the trainer profile card showing trainer name, principal ID, badges, wins, total battles, partner Pokémon, and gym leader progress fetched via custom query hooks.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility module providing functions to read URL/hash query parameters, persist them to sessionStorage, retrieve them, and clean sensitive params from the URL hash without page reload.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the default theme with anime/Pokémon-specific colors, fonts (Bangers, Nunito), custom shadows, border radii, and keyframe animations for battle UI effects.
project_state.json: Project state snapshot describing the Pokémon-style mobile RPG's architecture, existing features, known issues, required image assets, and overall design goals for a Play Store release.
